step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("fracture causes in gold dental restorations")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

You must write at most two sentences. Then state your clear answer choice using \boxed{}.
\boxed{D} I agree with the prior conclusion that fracture of gold restoration primarily depends on all of the above factors. The retrieved literature highlights that improperly designed restorations create stress concentration points, increasing fracture risk—even when made from strong alloys—as seen in cases of cracked tooth syndrome due to excessive occlusal load exceeding one quarter of intercuspal distance. Alloy composition and noble-to-base ratios contribute indirectly via affecting overall strength and resistance to deformation, supporting the idea that design, material selection, and interaction between them jointly determine failure mode.
